description: Array bisection algorithm. Provides functions to maintain a list in sorted order without having to sort the list after each insertion.

functions.name: bisect

params.name: a
params.description: A sorted list to bisect.

params.name: x
params.description: The value to locate an insertion point for.

params.name: lo
params.description: Lower bound of the subset of the list to consider.

params.name: hi
params.description: Upper bound of the subset of the list to consider.

returns.description: An insertion point which comes after (to the right of) any existing entries of x in a.

functions.description: Alias for bisect_right(). Locate the proper insertion point for x in a to maintain sorted order. The return value is suitable for use as the first parameter to list.insert().

functions.name: bisect_left

params.name: a
params.description: A sorted list to bisect.

params.name: x
params.description: The value to locate an insertion point for.

params.name: lo
params.description: Lower bound of the subset of the list to consider.

params.name: hi
params.description: Upper bound of the subset of the list to consider.

returns.description: An insertion point which comes before (to the left of) any existing entries of x in a.

functions.description: Locate the leftmost insertion point for x in a to maintain sorted order. The returned insertion point i partitions the array a into two halves so that all(val < x for val in a[lo:i]) for the left side and all(val >= x for val in a[i:hi]) for the right side.

functions.name: bisect_right

params.name: a
params.description: A sorted list to bisect.

params.name: x
params.description: The value to locate an insertion point for.

params.name: lo
params.description: Lower bound of the subset of the list to consider.

params.name: hi
params.description: Upper bound of the subset of the list to consider.

returns.description: An insertion point which comes after (to the right of) any existing entries of x in a.

functions.description: Locate the rightmost insertion point for x in a to maintain sorted order. The returned insertion point i partitions the array a into two halves so that all(val <= x for val in a[lo:i]) for the left side and all(val > x for val in a[i:hi]) for the right side.

functions.name: insort

params.name: a
params.description: A sorted list to insert into.

params.name: x
params.description: The value to insert.

params.name: lo
params.description: Lower bound of the subset of the list to consider.

params.name: hi
params.description: Upper bound of the subset of the list to consider.

functions.description: Alias for insort_right(). Insert x in a in sorted order. This is equivalent to a.insert(bisect.bisect_right(a, x, lo, hi), x).

functions.name: insort_left

params.name: a
params.description: A sorted list to insert into.

params.name: x
params.description: The value to insert.

params.name: lo
params.description: Lower bound of the subset of the list to consider.

params.name: hi
params.description: Upper bound of the subset of the list to consider.

functions.description: Insert x in a in sorted order. If x is already present in a, the insertion point will be before (to the left of) any existing entries. This is equivalent to a.insert(bisect.bisect_left(a, x, lo, hi), x).

functions.name: insort_right

params.name: a
params.description: A sorted list to insert into.

params.name: x
params.description: The value to insert.

params.name: lo
params.description: Lower bound of the subset of the list to consider.

params.name: hi
params.description: Upper bound of the subset of the list to consider.

functions.description: Insert x in a in sorted order. If x is already present in a, the insertion point will be after (to the right of) any existing entries. This is equivalent to a.insert(bisect.bisect_right(a, x, lo, hi), x).
